description:
You are working as an integration-test developer.  
    In the directory /home/user/api_tests you will find three existing log files that were produced by recent automated API regression runs:

        /home/user/api_tests/api1.log
        /home/user/api_tests/api2.log
        /home/user/api_tests/deprecated.log

    Every line inside those files follows the pattern

        ISO-8601-timestamp  SP  LEVEL  SP  HTTP-STATUS-CODE  SP  TEXT-MESSAGE  SP  URI

    Example (from api1.log):
        2023-07-01T12:00:00Z ERROR 500 INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR /v1/users/123

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Search every *.log file that lives directly inside /home/user/api_tests.  
    2. From each file, extract only the lines that contain the literal string  “ERROR”.  
    3. Aggregate those error lines into a single file called

            /home/user/api_tests/error_report.log

    Required report format  
    ----------------------  
    • One line per error line found.  
    • Each output line must be constructed as:

            <source-filename>:<original-line-number>:<original-line-text>

      where  
        <source-filename>     is the base name such as api1.log (no path),  
        <original-line-number> is the 1-based line number (as reported by grep -n),  
        <original-line-text>   is the full original line’s text with no leading or trailing spaces trimmed.

    • Lines must appear in ascending alphabetical order of the source filename.  
      Within each filename, preserve the original line order.

    • The file must end with a single Unix newline (LF) and no blank lines either before or after the data.

    Constraints  
    -----------  
    • Solve the task using find combined with xargs (or a single pipeline that reflects that usage).  
    • Do NOT open the files interactively; everything has to be done non-interactively via shell commands.  
    • Do NOT hard-code the line numbers or file contents; derive them programmatically.  
    • The only artefact you must create or modify is /home/user/api_tests/error_report.log.  
    • No other files should be created, deleted or moved.

    Verification hint  
    -----------------  
    Automated tests will:

      1. Check that /home/user/api_tests/error_report.log exists and is readable.  
      2. Compare its exact byte contents against the expected lines described above (strict match, including the final newline).  
      3. Ensure that no additional files were altered outside /home/user/api_tests.

    Once the report is generated, you are done.
truth:
Initial fixture – pre-existing files and their exact contents (each line ends with LF):

    1. /home/user/api_tests/api1.log
       2023-07-01T12:00:00Z ERROR 500 INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR /v1/users/123
       2023-07-01T12:00:02Z INFO 204 NO_CONTENT /v1/users/123

    2. /home/user/api_tests/api2.log
       2023-07-02T15:30:10Z ERROR 504 GATEWAY_TIMEOUT /v1/orders
       2023-07-02T15:31:10Z INFO 201 CREATED /v1/orders

    3. /home/user/api_tests/deprecated.log
       2023-06-30T10:00:00Z ERROR 410 GONE /v0/legacy

    Expected artefact after successful completion:

    File path: /home/user/api_tests/error_report.log  
    Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--) and owned by user.  
    Content (exact, with one LF at EOL):

        api1.log:1:2023-07-01T12:00:00Z ERROR 500 INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR /v1/users/123
        api2.log:1:2023-07-02T15:30:10Z ERROR 504 GATEWAY_TIMEOUT /v1/orders
        deprecated.log:1:2023-06-30T10:00:00Z ERROR 410 GONE /v0/legacy

    (There is a single newline character right after “…/v0/legacy”. No extra blank lines.)
name: task_000000_0fc28097
